feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Event Mesh
feed.subtitle: SAP Event Mesh Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Notification Error / Stop Automatic Event Mesh - S/4
entry.content: We currently have an issue with Event Mash. We are using the standard execution parameters, but at times it automatically turns off, interrupting the processes of sending integration to BTP/CPI and we do not receive any alert/notification of the problem, We need to understand the reason for the interruption and if there is a standard notification solution, in case this problem occurs

entry.title: Hello, May we know if the reported issue is fixed or still persists ?
entry.content: Hello, May we know if the reported issue is fixed or still persists ?

entry.title: Trigger event from SAP Document Management Service
entry.content: Is it possible to receive a notification / event / or call webhook when a file is uploaded / changed in SAP Document Management Service.

entry.title: Content-type when calling web hook from Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to call a an API on Service Now as a WebHook from SAP Event Mesh . The API only accepts the content-type application/json, but Event Mesh sets the content type to application/cloudevent+json, despite our settings.

We have configured the web hook to have a default content type of application/json, and I can see in the cloud event coming from S/4 that the content type is set as application/json

See attached screenshots of config and the relevant envelope.

What can we do make sure we always use application/json when calling this web hook?

Regards

Mattias

entry.title: Event Mesh activation in Integration Suite fails
entry.content:
Hi,

I added Manage Business Events (Event Mesh) from Manage Capabilities in Integration Suite and activated it in Configure -> Event Mesh and it shows Failed.

Even if I Deactivate the Event Mesh and reactivate it, it still shows Failed.
Please let me know if you know the cause or how to solve this problem.

Best Regards,
KuroKen.

entry.title: Processing messages from dead message queue of SAP event mesh
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

What is the best approach to process the messages from a dead message queue in SAP Event Mesh?

Does it make sense to me to create another iFlow to periodically read from DMQ using REST and process/put the message back to the main queue? Is there any manual way to put the messages from DMQ to main queue?

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Publish SAP Asset Performance Management Indicator Updates via Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hey, i am a little new to SAP - so please excuse inaccuracies. I have the following issue:

I want to connect my SAP Asset Performance Management Instance with my S4/Hana Instance
There is an Event Mesh and an APM instance in my SAP BTP cockpit already


Now as far as i understand it, the SAP Event Mesh only allows S4/Hana to publish information, but since my IoT Devices send their indicator information directly to APM,i am still looking for a way to transfer these IoT device Indicators from to my S4/Hana.
That is because S4 contains the rules that fire maintenance requests.

I know that there is a way to implement it manually, but if possible i want to stick to OOTB solutions.
To summarize: Is it possible to post SAP APM indicator updates to S4/Hana (e.g. via Event Mesh)?

entry.title: Cap application stops listen Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hi Experts

I have a CAP App with multi tenancy arch. And I am using Event Mesh with these configuration but while in application use somewhere in application is stopping listening the queue. When I restart the app, app is woking fine again.

Why this stituaion might happen, any idea?

"messaging": {
        "kind": "enterprise-messaging-shared",
        "subscribePrefix": "$namespace/",
        "queue": {
          "name": "$namespace/etransport-queue",
          "accessType": "NON_EXCLUSIVE"
        }
      }

entry.title: Integration Suite Event Mesh Capability - Programmatically query queues
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to know if there is a programmatic way to query queues (names, Queue Size, number of Messages Queued, number of Unacknowledged Messages) like by using an ODATA service.

We need to create an alert if messages stored in the queues are not being consumed by the iFlows, but right now, we couldn't find a way to do it, only a manual way.

Thanks

entry.title: Event Mesh IS Subscribe Access Denied
entry.content:
Hi, I'm trying to consume messages from a  SAP Event Mesh Integration Suite queue via HTTP. The exact same setup worked perfectly fine a few days ago.

Publishing via the HTTP endpoint (/messagingrest/v1/queues/{queue-name}/messages) works. But consuming the same message via the HTTP endpoint (/messagingrest/v1/queues/{queue-name}/messages/consumption) returns the following response:

{
    "error": {
        "code": "403",
        "message": "subscribe access denied"
    },
    "message": "subscribe access denied"
}

I tried creating a whole new EM instance + service key + queue (with/without namespace) + topic. Nothing seems to work.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Advanced Event Mesh for Central contracts
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are implementing Advanced Event Mesh to integrate with our S/4 HANA system for Central Purchase Contracts, following the guidelines from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub blog: https://api.sap.com/event/OP_CENTRALPURCHASECONTRACTEVENTS/resource.

To facilitate this, we have configured the following topics for publishing events related to Central Purchase Contracts:

  • (i) ce/sap/s4/beh/centralpurchasecontract/v1/CentralPurchaseContract/Changed/v1
  • (ii) ce/sap/s4/beh/centralpurchasecontract/v1/CentralPurchaseContract/Created/v1

These topics will receive events corresponding to changes and creation of Central Purchase Contracts, respectively.

We have also created a single queue to subscribe to both of these topics. This setup has been tested successfully by publishing messages to the specified topics using Postman.

Please see below screenshot that shows test is successful from postman and it returns 200 OK status.

 

 

 

Now, we are trying to create channel with topic endpoints on S4HANA backend but we get the following errors while trying to do so.

Would you be able to guide regarding this ? or kindly help me to know if someone already something similar to push the events for S/4HANA?

Please find below screenshots regarding Channel configuration on S/4 HANA backend.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Automatic sending of SalesOrder messages from the S/4 HANA Public Cloud to SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello Community,

When a new SalesOrder message is created or updated in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I would like this message to be automatically triggered in the SAP Event Mesh and stored in the salesorder queue of the SAP Event Mash. I have already stored an “event mesh” system with the corresponding host and outbound communication (OAuth 2.0 via clientID and clientsecret from the event mesh) in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud under Communication Systems. I have created a sales order queue directly in the event mesh under the corresponding message client and stored the topic “sap/s4/beh/salesorder/created/v1”. What steps still need to be taken explicitly in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud so that the sales orders are automatically sent to the queue?

In general, I later retrieve the contents of the queue from the cloud integration via an AMQP adapter and then the other SalesOrder information using an OData call.

Thanks in advance and best regards

Michael Seydler

entry.title: Configuring BTP Destination in Advanced Event Mesh for CAP Consumer
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am working on a proof of concept (PoC) where we have configured Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) to track changes in sales orders using the "Order Changed" event. After various initial configurations, we have successfully received messages in AEM when a sales order is modified.

Now, on the consumer side a CAP (Node.js) application has been developed, that can listen to the message queue. The CAP application has been set up and is secured with XSUAA.

To enable AEM to connect to the CAP endpoint, we have configured OAuth 2.0 in AEM. Everything is working fine. However, I was wondering if it is possible to configure a BTP Destination in AEM that directly points to the CAP endpoint.

Does AEM support this kind of integration via BTP Destinations? If so, what would be the recommended approach to achieve this? any relevant documentation links would also be helpful 

 

some further details

AEM is in a different subaccount then the consuming CAP applicaiton. I am not sure if this helps, that if I create a destination in BTP pointing to that AEM instance and then bind it to the CAP application, will I already start receiving messages?

entry.title: Conditionally send a standard event from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Cloud to BTP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am exploring the event mesh functionality on BTP Cloud Integration Suite. My scenarios is when a sales order item delivery date is changed, I need to send an event to BTP Event Mesh. I know standard event topic 'sap/s4/beh/salesorder/v1/SalesOrder/ItemChanged/v1' is available but it is sent for any change on sales order item. I think this cannot be achieved with mere filtering by any properties.

Is there any way I can influence the event publish from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to send only in case of change to delivery date if sales order item?

If not, the event consumer has to call s/4hana cloud api to get the sales order detail. In this case how can we know that delivery date has changed and not any other fields?

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.title: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a doubt related to event message consumption in SAP Cloud Integration. I am consuming an event topic configured in Event Mesh capability within SAP Integration Suite.

And created a topic subscription in the above queue. This event is sent from S/4HANA Cloud system when a sales order item is changed.

 

I am consuming the event in Cloud Integration process using AMQP sender adapter

 

 

Now when a sales order item is changed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I see the iFlow is triggered successfully but there always are two runs in the log:

Also the queue in event mesh is not cleared even after successful completion of the message in cloud integration process consuming the event.

1. Why is it triggering two runs of the same consumer integration process?

2. Why is the queue not getting cleared after integration flow completes.

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.title: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh of SAP Cloud Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a doubt related to event message consumption in SAP Cloud Integration. I am consuming an event topic configured in Event Mesh capability within SAP Integration Suite.

 

And created a topic subscription in the above queue. This event is sent from S/4HANA Cloud system when a sales order item is changed.

 

 

I am consuming the event in Cloud Integration process using AMQP sender adapter

 

 

 

 

Now when a sales order item is changed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I see the iFlow is triggered successfully but there always are two runs in the log:

 

Also the queue in event mesh is not cleared even after successful completion of the message in cloud integration process consuming the event.

 

1. Why is it triggering two runs of the same consumer integration process?

2. Why is the queue not getting cleared after integration flow completes.

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.title: Event Mesh v.s. Cloud Integration - JMS Adapter
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

My understanding: To achieve asynchronous system integration, Event Mesh is often used. However, according to a blog, asynchronous integration can also be achieved without Event Mesh by using the JMS Adapter within Cloud Integration.

Questions:

  1. Is my understanding correct?
  2. If my understanding is correct, what are the advantages and disadvantages of using Event Mesh versus Cloud Integration - JMS Adapter?
  3. Are there specific use cases for Event Mesh versus Cloud Integration - JMS Adapter? Explanations with examples are appreciated. (when to use event mesh, and when to use JMS Adapter?)
  4. When subscribing Services in BTP Cockpit, Event Mesh must be registered separately from Integration Suite. Does this mean the costs are calculated separately?

Thank you.

entry.title: How to send an event with SAP NetWeaver Add-On for Event enablement 1.0?
entry.content:
I followed the configuration steps of the SAP NetWeaver Add-On for Event enablement 1.0, as described here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP%20NetWeaver%20Add-On%20for%20Event%20enablement%201.0/e966e6c0e61443ebaa0270a4bae4b363/53d14e7af03f436a8dbf6072928ed4bf.html

I tried to send a notification event for Sales Order Change Event, as described here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-messaging-for-sap-erp-howto-guide-part-2-first-use-case/ba-p/13463694

Unfortunately, it does not work, even though I have done all the steps correctly (I suppose). Checking transactions /ASADEV/ACI_MONITOR or to SLG1 does not show me anything (also no errors). My questions is: What can I do now, to identify the problem? I checked all steps steps, in case I missed something and now I am unsure how my further error analysis shall look like. Would be great if someone can provide some help.

entry.title: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event based processes
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently following a codejam blog regarding event-based processes for sap build. here is the link of my current lesson: 3 - Explore SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh | SAP Tutorials

one of the prerequisite is a "participants handbook" which contains the system and credentials needed to follow the blog.

where can i get this? thanks

entry.title: Publishing to a Queue or Topic in BTP Event Mesh using Postman
entry.content:
Dear Professional.

Currently, I am creating and setting up BTP's Event Mesh Instance (only messagingrest method is activated) and testing in Cockpit, all (Publish - Queue/Topic, Cosume) are processed normally, but an error occurs when requesting Publish (Queue and Topic) in Postman.

When testing Publish (Queue) in Postman, a 403 Error occurs. I don't know what the problem is.

The URI called is as follows.
https://<http rest address>/messagingrest/v1/queues/sap%2Fdemo%2Fai/messages

Thank you.
